behaviors.description: A file placed inside a subdirectory of the watched root is processed, not only files placed directly in the root directory.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is real filesystem watcher recursion behavior (OS events + watcher library interaction), not a single function. MOCK-MISS: this bug appears when daemon/watcher/FS interact. REAL-INFRA and MOCK-HIDE: mocked watcher events can falsely pass even if recursive watching is broken in real OS notifications. LLM-DEP and MULTI-STEP are not applicable. Rejection gate: Integration rejected because behavior text requires actual watched-directory event propagation from subdirectories.
behaviors.test_description: Run the daemon against a real temp directory tree on the host OS, create `root/subdir/newfile`, and assert one transformed output appears without restart. This requires real watcher + real FS events.

behaviors.description: The deduplication logic correctly discards a second processing request for the same file path within a configurable time window.
behaviors.justification: Behavior is path+time-window decision logic and can fail entirely inside one local dedupe component. MOCK-MISS: no required cross-module boundary in the behavior text. REAL-INFRA/MOCK-HIDE: real OS/network/subprocess not needed to expose the core defect. LLM-DEP and MULTI-STEP are not applicable. Rejection gate: Integration/System rejected because behavior text is specifically about dedupe rule correctness for same path within a window, which is testable at pure logic boundary.
behaviors.test_description: Instantiate dedupe component with fake clock, submit same canonical path twice within window (second rejected), then after window (accepted).

behaviors.description: When the operating system emits multiple filesystem events for a single file write, the daemon processes the file exactly once.
behaviors.justification: Behavior explicitly names OS duplicate event emission, so failure boundary is OS watcher semantics. MOCK-MISS: multi-event collapse bug arises at watcher/daemon interaction. REAL-INFRA and MOCK-HIDE: synthetic single event or simplified fake can hide duplicate-event storms. LLM-DEP and MULTI-STEP are not applicable. Rejection gate: Integration rejected because behavior text requires handling actual OS-emitted duplicate filesystem events.
behaviors.test_description: With daemon running on real temp dir, perform one write pattern known to emit multiple events (create+append/rename sequence), then assert exactly one pipeline execution/output for that path.

behaviors.description: If the watched directory is removed and then recreated while the daemon is running, the daemon resumes watching the new directory without a restart.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is runtime OS filesystem lifecycle (delete/recreate inode/path) and watcher re-subscription logic. MOCK-MISS: bug appears when real watch handles are invalidated and must reattach. REAL-INFRA and MOCK-HIDE: in-process stubs typically do not model OS watch invalidation accurately. LLM-DEP and MULTI-STEP are not applicable. Rejection gate: Integration rejected because behavior text requires live delete/recreate while process is running.
behaviors.test_description: Start daemon watching real temp path, remove watched directory, recreate same path, drop a new file, and assert it is processed without restarting daemon.

behaviors.description: When multiple files arrive at the same time, they are processed in the order determined by their creation timestamps.
behaviors.justification: Primary failure boundary is cross-module handoff: intake queue + metadata lookup + scheduler ordering. IDEA-PROBE applies: this is an explicit ordering/state-propagation claim across modules, so Unit is too low. INTEGRATION-BRAKE checks: (a) no mandatory real OS/network/subprocess behavior is required if arrivals and timestamps are injected deterministically; (b) no AI/ML dependency; (c) no multi-agentic chain; (d) no LLM stages. Rejection gate: System rejected because behavior text does not require validating OS-specific watcher quirks, only ordering by creation timestamps.
behaviors.test_description: Wire ingestion service, metadata provider, and processing queue in-process; feed concurrent arrival requests with controlled creation timestamps and assert processing order follows timestamps.

behaviors.description: A file that is still being written to when the arrival event fires is not picked up for processing until writing is complete.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is real write-in-progress behavior and event timing on OS filesystem. MOCK-MISS: partial-write races appear only when writer process and watcher interact over real FS timing. REAL-INFRA and MOCK-HIDE: fakes often present atomic writes and hide premature pickup bugs. LLM-DEP and MULTI-STEP are not applicable. Rejection gate: Integration rejected because behavior text explicitly depends on write completion timing relative to arrival event.
behaviors.test_description: Use a real writer that streams/chunks data to a file while daemon is watching; assert daemon does not process during partial write and processes once after final flush/close.
